title: Law 416/2019 on the ban of the use of coal for energy production
description: On 27 February 2019, Finland adopted a law aiming to ban the use of coal for power generation after 1 May 2029. In addition, there are subsidies for companies that implement the change before 2026. The energy use of coal ended in Spring 2025, four years before the deadline of May 2029 as required by this law.
source: JOIN IEA/IRENA Policy and Measures Database

title: Reduction of Carbon Dioxide Emissions from Coal-fired Generation of Electricity Regulations
description:
In 2018, the Government of Canada announced amendments to the Reduction of Carbon Dioxide Emissions from Coal-fired Generation of Electricity Regulations (2012) to accelerate the phase-out of conventional coal-fired power generation from 2050 to 2030. The regulations do so by requiring all units to meet a performance standard of 420 t/GWh by December 31, 2029. Owners/operators can achieve the requirements by installing carbon capture equipment, switching to run on natural gas, or shutting down operations.
 
The Government of Canada can negotiate equivalency agreements “with provinces that have regulations capable of achieving equal or better emission-reduction outcomes compared to the federal regulations. These agreements would establish conditions under which the federal regulations would not apply in a given province, and provincial regulations would apply instead.

title: COP23 - Portugal's coal phase-out
description:
As part of its decarbonization strategy, and pledges in Bonn for COP23, Portugal pledges to phase-out coal use in the power sector by 2030 at the latest. 
The target was achieved in November 2021 by closing the Pego and Sines power plants.

title: Decarbonization plan for the electrical system
description:
The government released a plan detailing the coal phase out of the electrical system, a technology that adds up to over 5 GW of installed capacity in Chile. The plan is made in agreement with the coal-fired plant owners/operators. The objective is to phase out or reconvert all coal-fired power plants no later than 2040. 
The first phase, planned to be completed by 2024, will decomission 11 units (31% of installed capacity, 1731 MW).
technologies: ["Combined heat and power (CHP)","Coal","Electricity generation and CHP technologies"]

title: Act to Reduce and End Coal-Fired Power Generation
description:
The Act to Reduce and End Coal-Fired Power Generation aims at gradually reducing and eventually phasing out from hard coal and lignite power generation. Its goal is to phase out from coal in a socially responsible and economically viable manner. It thus created an adaptation fund as well as new state funding guidelines for energy-intensive businesses. It also created a funding programme for GHG neutral heat generation and use.  

The schedule is as follows: 
- Reach 15 GW of hard coal and 15 GW of lignite power generation capacity in 2022
- Reach 8 GW of hard coal and 9 GW of lignite power generation capacity in 2030
- The rest must be decommissioned by 2038. 

The law stipulates several years in which the government will evaluate whether the decommissioning can be moved up to 2035. 

The federal government introduced tenders for the decommissioning of hard coal-fired power plants. In seven tendering phases more than 40 bids were awarded a total amount of more than 10 GW, to be closed gradually by 2026. The German government also committed to cover a total of 4.35 billion EUR in payments to utilities for the country's operators of lignite-fired power plants. This would accelerate the shutdown of their assets and waive operator's rights for future lawsuits once they agree to receive the payments.

In 2021, the German government considered that an earlier coal phase out was possible, leading the government to envision an exit from coal before 2038. 

In 2022, the Federal Government, in agreement with the operator, accelerated the phase-out of lignite in the Rhenish mining area from 2038 to 2030.
technologies: ["Combustion-based power generation","Coal","Electricity generation and CHP technologies"]

title: NECP - Final retirement of coal-fired power generation plants
description:
The government announced the retiring of the following lignite power generation plants by 2023 (3,912‬ MW in total):
  • Kardia 1&2 in 2019 (550 MW)
  • Amyntaio 1&2 in 2020 (546 MW)
  • Kardia 3&4 in 2021 (560 MW)
  • Agios Dimitrios 1,2,3&4 and Megalopolis 3 in 2022 (1 369 MW)
  • Agios Dimitrios 5, Florina/Meliti, Megalopolis 4 in 2023 (887 MW)

The last coal-fired unit, Ptolemaida V, is still under construction and has a capacity of 660 MW. Its phase-out is set for 2025, after which it will be converted to a natural gas plant.
technologies: ["Combustion-based power generation","Coal","Electricity generation and CHP technologies"]

title: Bill on the prohibition of coal-fired thermoelectric plants
description:
The Bill was drafted and presented in January 2020. It recognizes the impacts of emissions from coal-fired power plants as a risk to human health and to the environment. On June 22, 2021, the Bill was approved by the Chamber of Deputies.
 
The installation and operation of coal-fired thermoelectric plants will be prohibited throughout the country. For power plants that are less than 30 years old, the ban will take effect on December 31, 2025.
technologies: ["Combustion-based power generation","Electricity generation and CHP technologies","Coal"]

title: Carbon Neutrality Vision and Strategy for Industry and Energy
description:
Korea announced its first comprehensive strategy to achieve carbon neutrality in the industry and energy sectors, "Carbon Neutrality Vision and Strategy for Industry and Energy". 

The vision named "Manufacturing Renaissance 2.0" is the No.4 industrial powerhouse to lead a low-carbon economy, with 5 objectives aimed for 2050. 
1) Expand share of renewables to 70.8%
2) Increase self-sufficiency rate of clean hydrogen to 60%
3) Expand share of high value-added, eco-friendly items to 84.1%
4) Reduce carbon intensity in the manufacturing industry to 68tCO2eq/KRW billion
5) Improve export ranking to 4th.

The government vowed all-out support with tax, finance, and regulatory innovation for firms to phase-out coal power generation by 2050 and achieve a carbon-free energy mix with renewables and hydrogen. Industrial R&D projects focused on carbon-neutral technologies, environment-friendly infrastructure, low-carbon materials, parts and equipment, and green engineering will be pushed ahead as well.
technologies: ["CO2 Capture","CO2 capture and transport technologies","Electricity generation and CHP technologies","Hydrogen","Hyrogen fuel cells"]

title: Updated National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP) - Coal phase-out target
description:
The Czech government set a coal phase-out target by 2033 in its 2023 updated NECP. 
Czechia also joined the Powering Past Coal Alliance in the same year.

title: Integrated National Energy and Climate Plan for Austria - coal phase-out
description:
Austria closed in 2020 the Mellach district heating power plant, which was the last coal power plant in operation in the country.
This coal phase-out stems from the broader objective of the government to reach 100% low-carbon electricity by 2030, quoted in its last National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP).

title: National Climate Law - Coal phase-out
description: Greece aims to support its climate neutrality pledge by 2050 by completely phasing-out solid fossil fuels, including coal, by 31 Dec 2028.

title: Climate Act - Denmark's coal phase-out
description:
Denmark is set to ban coal-based electricity production by 2028, closing the Nordjyllandsværket power station by this date. 
This phase-out goes along with the repeated commitment of reducing by 70% GHG emissions by 2030 compared to 1990 levels set in the Climate Act in 2020.

title: Climate Action Plan 2019 - Ireland's coal phase-out
description:
Ireland aims to phase out electricity generation from coal by closing the Moneypoint power plant by 2025. 
This coal phase-out stems from the Climate Action Plan 2019.

title: Coal Ban Act
description: The Netherlands legislated in 2019 to prohibit the electricity production from coal beyond 2030.

title: National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP) - Spain's coal phase-out
description:
Spain sees its electricity production from coal to phase out by 2025. 
This coal phase-out is framed with region-specific just transitions policies to prevent its underlying socioeconomic effects.

title: Ukraine's coal phase out
description:
Ukraine's Energy Strategy towards 2050 enforces the commitment to phase out coal use from power generation by 2035. 
This pledge confirms the position of Ukraine during CO26 in Glasgow in 2021.

title: 2019 National Energy and Climate Plan - Coal phase-out
description:
In the NECP submitted to the European Commission in 2019, Hungary aims to phase out coal and lignite from domestic electricity production by 2030 at the latest, in order to reach its emissions reduction objective by at least 40% by the same date compared to 1990. 
The target aligns with the updated NECP updated in 2023.

title: Just Energy Transition Partnership - Coal phase-out
description: Indonesia committed to achieving net-zero by 2060 at the latest, supported by complete coal-phase out by 2056. Furthermore, Indonesia has plans to bring forward its coal phase-out target to 2050 if funding is made available to Indonesia.

title: Territorial Just Transition Plan (TJTP) - coal phase-out
description:
Slovak Republic committed to phase out coal by 2030 as part of their environmental policy strategy implemented in 2017. 
The electricity and heat generation from lignite ppower plant Nováky  ended on December 20, 2023. The Vojany power plant was decommissioned in March 2024.

title: Sweden's coal phase-out
description:
Sweden closed its last coal power plant, Värtaverket, two years before its original plan i.e. 2020.
The city of Stockholm, half owner of the plant, led this decision in December 2019.

title: United Kingdom's coal phase-out strategy
description:
In 2017, the United Kingdom committed to phasing-out coal in the power sector by 2025. 
Since 2021, the United Kingdom government has brought forward its target to 1 October 2024, in line with its net zero climate strategy.

title: COP28 - USA coal phase-out
description:
At COP28, the US Climate Envoy John Kerry mentionned the need to phase-out coal to reach the pledge to reach net zero-emissions by 2035 in the power sector.
The United States also joined the Powering Past Coal Alliance in the same period of time.

title: PPCA - Colombia's coal phase-out
description:
In September 2023, Colombia joined the Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA), pledging to stop building new unabated coal power plants and phasing out unabated coal use in the power sector.
No statement over the enforcement year of this phase-out have been announced yet.

title: COP26 "End of Coal Sight" initiative
description: More than 40 countries pledged to phase out coal use for power generation at COP26, with at least 23 countries making new commitments. No clear common target date has been agreed for these pledges.

title: PPCA - Mexico's coal phase-out
description:
Mexico is a member of the Power Past Coal Alliance (PCCA) since 2017, which implies a phase-out from unabated coal by 2030 in OECD and EU countries. 
No plan supports a federal strategy as of 2023.

title: PPCA - Morocco's coal phase-out
description:
Morocco joined the Powering Past Coal Alliance in 2023 which pledges to phase-out coal use in the power sector by 2030 in advanced economies, and 2040 for emerging markets and developping economies. 
No target year has been explicitly announced yet.

title: French strategy for energy and climate - coal phase-out
description:
The French strategy for energy and climate summarises the main objectives for the decade to phase down the dependance to fossil fuels. 
This notably includes a phase-out of coal use in the power sector by 2027.

title: National Energy Strategy (SEN) 2017 - coal phase-out
description: Coal phase out was first introduced in the National Energy Strategy (SEN) in 2017. Gradual cessation of coal-fired electricity generation by 2028 for approximately 5 GW, offset by strong growth in renewable energy and a plan for infrastructure interventions (flexible generation, networks and storage systems). Despite a slowdown in the process linked to the 2021-2022 energy crisis and temporary maximization of production made necessary by the energy crisis following the Russian-Ukrainian conflict, the phase-out is confirmed within the limits of security of supply needs and provided that replacement plants and the necessary infrastructure are built in time. The actions already implemented and planned are adequate to allow the phase-out of coal-fired power plants still in operation on the Continent. For Sardinia, however, the development of RES, storage and new interconnections with the continent (Tyrrhenian link, SACOI 3) are essential to guarantee the technical safety conditions of the network needed to complete the abandonment of coal in electricity production (total approximately 1,000 MW) and to date there are technical difficulties in fully achieving this objective by 2025.

title: National Resilience and Recovery Plan (NNRP) - coal phase-out
description: In its National Resilience and Recovery Plan, the Bulgarian government announced the phase-out of coal use in the electricity sector by 2038 or 2040.

title: Israel's updated Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) - coal phase-out
description: In its updated first NDC, Israel committed to a complete phase out of coal-fired power generation by 2026.

title: PPCA - Singapore's coal phase out
description: By joining the Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA) in 2021, Singapore committed to phasing out the use of unabated coal in its electricity mix by 2050, and to restrict direct government finance of unabated coal power internationally.

title: Thailand long-term low emission development strategy - coal phase-out
description:
In its long-term strategy (LT-LEDS) submitted to the UNFCCC, Thailand set a timeline to reach carbon neutrality by 2050 and climate neutrality by 2065.
This strategy includes a phase-out of coal in the electricity sector by 2050.

title: Belgium coal phase-out
description: The last active coal power plant, Langerlo, was converted to burn biomass from 2017 onwards.

title: LAW no. 2019-1147 relating to energy and climate - coal phase-out
description:
The French government promulgated in 2019 a law relating to energy and climate, setting in a legal framework key milestones and objectives to reach climate neutrality by 2050. 

This includes the shutdown of coal-fired power plants by January 1st, 2022.

title: G7 Climate, Energy and Environment Ministers’ Meeting Communiqué - coal phase-out
description: In the communiqué steming from the G7 Ministers' meeting in Italy, G7 members commits to phase out existing unabated coal power generation in their energy systems during the first half of 2030s or in a timeline consistent with keeping a limit of 1.5°C temperature rise within reach, in line with countries’ net-zero pathways.

title: COP26 - Croatia coal phase-out
description: At COP26, Croatia pledged to phase out the use of coal for power generation by 2033 at the latest.

title: Law n°334 regarding the decarbonisation of the energy sector
description: The law sets in Romanian legislation the phased out of available installed power generation capacity based on lignite and coal by 31 December 2032 at the latest.

title: 2023 Updated national energy and climate plan (NECP) - coal phase-out
description: In its updated national energy and climate plan submitted to the European Commission, Slovenia pledged to phasing out of domestic and imported coal for energy purposes and the cessation of operation of all coal units by 2033 at the latest.

title: Decision No. 266/QD-TTg approving Global Coal-to-Clean Power Transition Statement
description: The plan establishes targets and objectives to phase out coal for power generation split in three phases (2025- 2030; 2031 - 2040; 2041 - 2050), with the objective to decommissioning and using carbon capture for current installations.

title: G20 summit - coal phase out announcement
description: President Prabowo Subianto announced a plan to phase out coal-fired and all fossil-fueled power plants within the next 15 years, in 2040.
